steps.title: Identify y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at $x = 0$, where $y = -1$. So the y-intercept is $b = -1$.

steps.title: Find the slope using two points
steps.content:
From the graph, the line passes through $(0, -1)$ and $(1, -6)$.

$$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{-6 - (-1)}{1 - 0} = \frac{-5}{1} = -5$$

steps.title: Write the equation and match to the choices
steps.content:
With slope $m = -5$ and y-intercept $b = -1$, the equation is:

$$f(x) = -5x - 1$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
